pubmed_links: [Hoekstra et al. (2013) LMX1B forms transcriptional complex with PSPC1 and PSF in developing dopaminergic neurons](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/23300262/);[Tyzack et al. (2020) Paraspeckle components NONO and PSPC1 are not mislocalized from motor neuron nuclei in sporadic ALS](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/32844195/);[Hao et al. (2025) Paraspeckle Component 1: a multifunctional RNA binding protein](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/40520856/)
summary: PSPC1, a paraspeckle component and DBHS family RNA-binding protein, has been identified as part of a transcriptional complex with LMX1B and PSF expressed in developing mesodiencephalic dopaminergic neurons, linking it to dopaminergic differentiation pathways relevant to schizophrenia and mood disorders; additionally, PSPC1 expression is upregulated in fibroblasts from patients with NONO-related X-linked intellectual disability (MRXS34), suggesting compensatory or dysregulatory roles in neurodevelopmental disease. (novel candidate)
